publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-09 23:23:16 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The Morse Chain Company was incorporated in 1898 and manufactured power transmission equipment. Its subsidiaries, the Thomas Morse Aircraft Corporation (founded in 1917), the Peters-Morse Manufacturing Company (founded in 1919), and the Barr-Morse Corporation (founded in 1921, manufactured aircrafts, adding machines, and typewriters, respectively. The collection contains business records documenting the transactions of the Morse Chain Company and its subsidiary companies, dating circa 1915-1975.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Morse Chain Company records contains business records documenting the transactions of the Morse Chain Company and its subsidiary companies: the Thomas Morse Aircraft Corporation, the Barr-Morse Corporation, and the Peters-Morse Manufacturing Corporation, dating circa 1915-1975. The collection also contains bound volumes of the company newsletter the South Hill Echo and Re-Echo, and a poster: "The 'Rocker Joint,' Morse Genuine Silent Chains, from Automotive Industries, June 26, 1924.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Morse Chain Company. Records, #6889 . Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Morse Manufacturing Company was established in 1892 and incorporated in 1898 in Trumansburg, NY as the Morse Chain Company. The company manufactured bicycle chains and went on to develop chain and power transmission equipment from its Ithaca, NY factory, built in 1906. Its subsidiaries, the Thomas Morse Aircraft Corporation (founded in 1917), the Peters-Morse Manufacturing Company (founded in 1919), and the Barr-Morse Corporation (founded in 1921, manufactured aircrafts, adding machines, and typewriters, respectively. The Morse Chain Company was taken over by the Borg-Warner Corporation in 1929.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: No original order. Files arranged intellectually in alphabetical order.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hawley was President/General Manager at Morse Chain Co., having started out as a Sales Engineer 30 years earlier in 1916.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Bailliere chiefly kept his files organized by year and in alphabetical order. Folders not in alphabetical order have been intellectually interfiled.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bailliere was the Treasurer and Controller at Morse Chain Company from apporoximately the late 1940s until circa 1960.

Forecast of inventory, sales, and net income, F&A-5-C & F&A-5-D

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains organizational chart of the Morse Chain Company's Controller's Department.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes "corresondence books, automotive status reports, burden expense (1958-1959)" per original box label.

Forecast of inventory, sales, and net income, F&A-5-C & F&A-5-D

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains organizational chart of the Morse Chain Company's Controller's Department.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folders arranged in alphabetical order. Folders found misfiled have been intellectually interfiled.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Leboffe replaced Marion V. Bailliere Jr. as Treasurer and Controller at Morse Chain Company circa 1960, and held the position until circa 1971.

did.unittitle: American Sprocket Chain Manufacturing Assoc. ADL (A.D. Leboffe)
Assoc. of Worm Gear Speed Reducer Mfgrs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically. Folders not in alphabetical order have been intellectually interfiled.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Original filing cabinet labelled "1950 - Controller's Dept. files". Files appear to have belonged to V.P. Burgess
